response.content: Every 30 minutes in UTC, create a routine that checks my Gmail inbox and adds any new emails from a near.ai address to my Google Sheet called ABC. Do not run the inbox check now.

arguments.name: Check Gmail for near.ai emails → ABC Sheet
arguments.prompt: Check the Gmail inbox for any emails from a near.ai address (i.e. sender email ends with @near.ai). For each matching email found, append a new row to the Google Sheet called "ABC" with the following columns: Date/Time Received, Sender, Subject, and a short Snippet of the email body. Do not send any emails or modify the messages — read only. If no near.ai emails are found, do nothing.

response.content:
The routine is set up and active. Here's a summary:

**Trigger: "Check Gmail for near.ai emails → ABC Sheet"**

| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| **Schedule** | Every 30 minutes (`:00` and `:30`) in UTC |
| **Next run** | 2026-06-19T18:30:00Z |
| **What it does** | Searches Gmail inbox for emails from any `@near.ai` sender |
| **On match** | Appends a row to your Google Sheet **"ABC"** with: Date/Time Received, Sender, Subject, Snippet |
| **On no match** | Does nothing |

> **Note:** When the trigger first fires, make sure your "ABC" sheet has headers in place (e.g. `Date/Time Received`, `Sender`, `Subject`, `Snippet`). If you'd like me to add those headers to the sheet now, just say the word!
